channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: NY Republican: Putin is ‘the only one that won’ in Trump-Zelensky meeting
item.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) has called for President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky to quickly resolve their disagreements after the explosive White House meeting, which he believes was detrimental to Ukraine's interests.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Trump-Zelensky clash highlights Congress at a crossroads over foreign policy
item.description: The explosive clash between President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the White House on Friday has sparked a firestorm on Capitol Hill, pitting “America First” Republicans against Ukraine backers from both parties in the ferocious battle over the future of U.S. foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: World leaders back Zelenskyy following Trump, Vance Oval Office spat
item.description: World leaders pledge support for Ukraine and emphasize Russia is the "aggressor" following the Oval Office spat in which the Trump administration accused President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of being "disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European leaders jump to Zelensky's defense after contentious meeting with Trump
item.description: European leaders jumped to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s defense after his contentious meeting with President Trump in Washington that started with a warm tone but derailed swiftly.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Rose Girone, oldest living Holocaust survivor, dies at 113
item.description: Rose Girone, the oldest living survivor of the Holocaust and advocate for sharing the stories of survivors, died on Monday in New York at age 113.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Getting the Israel-Hamas ceasefire to the next phase will be difficult. Here's why
item.description: Israel and Hamas have begun working to advance their ceasefire agreement in Gaza to the next phase, but it's unclear if they'll get there and, if not, what comes next.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Violent protests erupt in Greece on anniversary of deadly train crash
item.description: A protest over the Greek government’s response to the nation’s largest ever train crash turned violent on Friday as Scores of youths hurled rocks and gasoline bombs at police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope suffers breathing crisis amid pneumonia battle, Vatican says
item.description: Following the episode, the pope began non-invasive ventilation and was responding well, according to the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope had a bronchial spasm and his prognosis remains guarded, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bodies of 12 Pakistani migrants who died in boat sinking have been repatriated
item.description: Officials say the bodies of 12 out of at least 16 Pakistanis who died this month when a boat carrying dozens of Europe-bound migrants sank off Libya’s coast have been repatriated to Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Myanmar online news site says its jailed journalist is tortured for revealing abuses of prisoners
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An independent online news agency in Myanmar said Friday that one of its journalists arrested two years ago has been subjected to daily physical and mental abuse after exposing human rights violations in the country’s main prison where he's being held.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Freight train derails in Czech Republic, causing major chemical fire
item.description: Czech firefighters are battling a major blaze in eastern Czech Republic after a freight train derailed and carcinogenic benzene from damaged tank wagons caught fire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bangladeshi students who led uprising that ousted ex-premier Sheikh Hasina form new political party
item.description: Students in Bangladesh who led a mass uprising to topple former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina in August are now diving into politics by forming a new political party.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Embassy in Thailand warns Americans of 'violent retaliatory attacks' risk after Uyghurs deported to China
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Bangkok is warning Americans of potential "violent retaliatory attacks" after a group of Uyghurs were deported by Thailand to China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN talks end in Rome with nations backing $200 billion a year plan to protect nature
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Global negotiators concluded an extended session of the United Nations biodiversity conference, COP16, with key commitments on funds needed and the institutions through which the funds will be channeled to protect the world's biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mourners bury one of the last hostages released from Gaza as talks start for ceasefire future
item.description: Mourners buried the remains of one of the last hostages released in the first phase of the ceasefire between Hamas militants and Israel.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis' condition improving but will remain 'guarded' until he is stable for several days: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is improving after spending the past two weeks hospitalized with double pneumonia, but the Vatican said his prognosis is "guarded" until he's stable for a few more days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A good rapport but mixed signals on Ukraine: Takeaways from Keir Starmer's trip to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prime Minister Keir Starmer wore a broad smile aboard the plane home from Washington. He landed back in Britain on Friday with the satisfaction of a tricky mission not quite accomplished, but off to a flying start.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Morocco urges people to not buy sheep for Eid al-Adha celebrations
item.description: OULAD SLAMA, Morocco (AP) — Sheep come running when Larbi El Ghazouani pours alfalfa and straw into their troughs twice a day.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and AKRAM OUBACHIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suicide bomber kills senior cleric at pro-Taliban seminary in northwest Pakistan on eve of Ramadan
item.description: Police in northwestern Pakistan say a suicide bomb attack on a mosque at a pro-Taliban seminary has killed a senior cleric and five other worshippers and wounded many other people ahead of the fasting month of Ramadan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin 'doesn't want peace,' EU chief diplomat warns ahead of Trump, Zelenskyy deal
item.description: The EU's chief diplomat has warned President Trump that Russian President Putin "doesn't really want peace" as Trump heads into a deal with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama and Costa Rica turning into a 'black hole' for migrants and deportees from US, observers warn
item.description: MIRAMAR, Panama (AP) — Officials in Costa Rica and Panama are confiscating migrants’ passports and cellphones, denying them access to legal services and moving them between remote outposts as they wrestle with the logistics of a suddenly reversed migration flow.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MATÍAS DELACROIX and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge overthrows conviction of owners of New Zealand island where 22 died in volcanic eruption
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — The owners of an island volcano in New Zealand where 22 tourists and local guides died in an eruption had their criminal conviction for failing to keep visitors safe thrown out by a judge on Friday.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico extradites dozens of cartel leaders and members to US, including drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
item.description: As Mexico and the U.S. negotiate trade tariffs, the Mexican government has handed over 29 cartel leaders and members who were wanted in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean births increased last year for the first time in nearly a decade
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The number of babies born in South Korea rebounded for the first time in nine years in 2024, welcome news for a country grappling with one of the the world’s most serious demographic crises.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US wants to end Ukraine war with little involvement: Ex-ambassador
item.description: President Donald Trump has met with European leaders during the week to discuss ending the war quickly.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis' condition continues to improve as he receives oxygen therapy: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is improving as he undergoes high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said in a statement. The 88-year-old pontiff has been hospitalized for nearly two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis continued to improve from double pneumonia Thursday, the Vatican said, working from his hospital room and going to his private chapel to pray, though doctors said he needs more days of “clinical stability” before they revise their guarded prognosis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian and US diplomats meet in Istanbul for second round of talks
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Russian and U.S. diplomats met in Istanbul on Thursday to discuss normalizing the operation of their respective embassies after years expelling each others' diplomats.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Female American tourist, 37, goes missing during jet ski ride in Thailand: report
item.description: A 37-year-old American tourist reportedly vanished Thursday in Thailand while jet skiing along Patong Beach near Phuket, prompting a search, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis' slight kidney insufficiency has receded as he battles pneumonia, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis slept well and was resting Thursday morning as his doctors say the 88-year-old pontiff has shown further slight improvement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's refusal to withdraw from this narrow strip of desert could threaten the Gaza ceasefire
item.description: Israel's refusal to withdraw from a narrow strip of desert on the Gaza side of the border with Egypt, as called for in the ceasefire with Hamas, could further threaten the fragile truce.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The EU beat COVID and ditched Russian energy. Now it must learn to live without US security help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Over the last five years, European Union countries have been forced to adapt to unprecedented circumstances. They pulled together to purchase tens of millions of vaccines and devised an innovative debt financing scheme to resuscitate their COVID-19-ravaged economies.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews: Investigators
item.description: The suspect arrived in Germany in 2023 as an unaccompanied minor and successfully applied for asylum, investigators said. He lives in Leipzig.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
